title: Light Portuguese Malt Loaf
description: A shaggy, light Portuguese-inspired dish featuring tahini paste, with a crusty wood-fired-bake finish. Perfect for a stress-free dinner or snack.

ingredients.name: tahini paste
ingredients.notes: make sure it's well stirred before using

ingredients.name: olive oil
ingredients.notes: use a high-quality oil for best flavor

ingredients.name: sugar
ingredients.notes: use a moderate amount for balance

ingredients.name: chopped fresh rosemary
ingredients.notes: freshly chopped is a must for best flavor

instructions: Preheat a wood-fired oven or conventional oven to 375°F (190°C).
Gather ingredients and equipment before starting to mix the dough.
Combine flours, tahini paste, sugar, salt, and rosemary in a large bowl.
Mix in olive oil, eggs, and plain yogurt until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover it with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Punch down the dough and shape it into a round loaf.
Place the loaf on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch of space around it.
Brush the top of the loaf with olive oil and sprinkle with coarse salt.
Bake the loaf in the preheated oven for 35 minutes, or until it's golden brown and sounds hollow when tapped.
Let the loaf cool on a wire rack for 10 minutes before slicing.
Slice the loaf and serve warm.
